title: Plan for 6 Incremental Improvements

steps.action: Confirm scope & constraints: confirm what 6 incremental improvements target (product, process, codebase), success metrics, timeline, and available resources.
Identify & prioritize: list candidate improvements, estimate effort & impact, and select the six highest-priority items with owners and acceptance criteria.

Implement iteratively: for each improvement, run a short cycle (design -> implement -> test -> review), deploying or merging only after passing acceptance criteria; work on items in priority order or parallel within resource limits.
Measure & iterate: collect metrics vs baseline, validate improvement; if targets aren’t met, refine or roll back; obtain stakeholder sign-off and document outcomes.

success_criteria: All six improvements are documented with owners and acceptance criteria.
Each improvement has been implemented or prototyped and validated against its acceptance criteria.
Automated or manual tests for implemented items pass and any regressions are addressed.
Relevant metrics show measurable improvement (or a documented rationale for deviations) and stakeholders sign off.
required_inputs: Clarification of target area(s) for improvements (product/process/codebase).
Baseline metrics or KPIs to measure impact.
Constraints: deadline, team capacity, tech constraints, and risk tolerances.
Stakeholders and owners for each improvement and access to necessary repositories/environments.
assumptions: means they want a concrete execution plan to implement six prioritized improvements.
No additional constraints were provided; plan includes an initial clarification step to avoid incorrect assumptions.
